tax_amount.description: Total tax collected on the product's sales for the date. Stored as nvarchar; expected numeric.
tax_amount.analysis_use: Aggregate to report tax liabilities, compute effective tax rate (tax_amount / gross_sales or tax_amount / net_sales), and reconcile tax across channels or with tax reporting tables.
business_questions: How much tax was collected by product for the month?
What is the effective tax rate applied to product X over the quarter?
tax_amount.nl2sql_hints: Cast to numeric and handle nulls. Ensure the tax convention (included/excluded) is understood—some systems may report tax on gross or net. Use consistent casting and rounding rules in NL2SQL queries to match finance reporting precision.

net_sales.description: Net sales amount after discounts and taxes for the product on the date. Stored as nvarchar; expected numeric and typically equals gross_sales - discount_amount + tax_amount (validate).
net_sales.analysis_use: Primary revenue metric for performance reporting (SUM, AVG). Used to rank products, compute contribution margins when combined with cost data, and trend analyses. Also used for reconciliation with ledger/invoice data.
business_questions: Which products contributed most to net sales last month?
What is the 7-day moving average of net sales for product X?
net_sales.nl2sql_hints: Cast to numeric. Because this is often the final reported metric, validate consistency with component columns. Avoid relying on string comparisons; parse to numeric early in NL2SQL. Consider pre-validating rows where numeric arithmetic between gross_sales, discount_amount and tax_amount doesn't match net_sales and flag them in diagnostics.

pos_sku.description: Point-of-sale specific SKU identifier for the product variant (e.g., APP009_001).
pos_sku.analysis_use: Primary lookup key within this table for filtering and grouping. Use for joins to POS transaction lines if transaction table stores pos_sku. Good for deduplication and item-level reporting.
business_questions: Which transactions (or stock entries) relate to this pos_sku?
What is the current price and stock for pos_sku APP009_001?
pos_sku.nl2sql_hints: Treat as text key. Trim whitespace and normalize case before equality comparisons. If joining to other tables use the exact pos_sku string; consider creating an index on pos_sku in production. Watch for NULLs (23%).

sku.description: Canonical SKU code likely representing product family or base SKU without variant suffix (e.g., APP009).
sku.analysis_use: Group-level aggregation and join key to product catalog tables. Useful for rollups from variants to base product and for joining external product metadata tables that use sku.
business_questions: What is the total stock across variants for SKU APP009?
What are the distinct variants available for SKU BRN007?
sku.nl2sql_hints: Also nvarchar; normalize before joins. Some systems may store variant mapping as sku + variant suffix; use string functions (like LEFT or SPLIT) carefully. Consider casting to consistent type in queries. Distinct count small (10).

store_id.description: Identifier for the store the POS product record belongs to (STORE_AHD_001).
store_id.analysis_use: Filter by store when running queries; used for multi-store joins and partition keys in larger datasets. Here constant, so filtering will not reduce rows.
business_questions: Which SKUs are available in store STORE_AHD_001?
How many active products are listed for each store?
store_id.nl2sql_hints: Constant in this dataset. Ensure joins that expect multiple stores handle the single-value case. Use store_id as a partition filter in production datasets; no selectivity here so queries filtering on store_id will not improve performance on this small sample.

plucode.description: PLU code for weighted or produce items (point-of-sale item code). Values appear with decimal-like formatting (e.g., 4011.0).
plucode.analysis_use: Lookup or grouping by PLU; cross-reference to PLU-based product lists. Could be cast to numeric or integer for consistent comparisons.
business_questions: Which PLU codes map to specific SKUs and product names?
How many products share the same PLU code?
plucode.nl2sql_hints: Stored as text with comma/decimal formatting. Cast to numeric/int if numeric comparisons or range filters are needed: try TRY_CAST(PLUCODE AS INT) or TRIM/REPLACE to remove '.0'. Beware of NULLs. Use exact string equality if other systems store PLU as string.

name.analysis_use: For display and grouping in reports, free-text filtering (ILIKE/LIKE) and fuzzy matching with other product name sources. Useful for user-facing queries.
business_questions: Show product names and their POS prices for customer display.
Which product names contain 'Apple' or 'Brinjal'?
name.nl2sql_hints: Text field; use LIKE/ILIKE or full-text search for partial matches. Consider normalization (trim, lower) for comparisons. Watch for duplicates where different pos_sku map to the same name.

uom.description: Unit of measure for the product (e.g., kg, 250g, 500g).
uom.analysis_use: Group and normalize measures; used to convert quantities to a common unit for aggregation and comparisons. Filter to specific packaging sizes.
business_questions: How many SKUs are measured in kg vs packaged weights?
What is the stock quantity by unit of measure?
uom.nl2sql_hints: Low cardinality; treat as categorical. When aggregating quantities, ensure UOM conversion logic exists. Use equality filters on exact strings; consider mapping table for conversions when combining different UOMs.

pos_price.description: Price charged at POS as text (e.g., '185.0'). Represents unit selling price.
pos_price.analysis_use: Numeric aggregation (average, min, max), revenue estimates when multiplied by quantities, and price comparisons. Must be cast to numeric for arithmetic.
business_questions: What is the average and median POS price across SKUs?
Which SKUs have a price greater than 100?
pos_price.nl2sql_hints: Store as nvarchar: always CAST or TRY_CAST to DECIMAL/NUMERIC in queries before arithmetic (e.g., TRY_CAST(pos_price AS DECIMAL(10,2))). Handle values with trailing '.0'. NULLs present (23%).

tax_rate.description: Tax rate applicable to the product as text; constant 5.0 in this snapshot.
tax_rate.analysis_use: Tax calculations and price breakdowns (tax-exclusive vs tax-inclusive pricing). Low cardinality (constant) so rarely useful for grouping here but important for financial calculations.
business_questions: What tax amount applies to each SKU's POS price?
What is the revenue split by tax rate?
tax_rate.nl2sql_hints: Cast to numeric for computations. Since it's constant in this sample, avoid grouping by tax_rate unless expanding to larger dataset. Use TRY_CAST(tax_rate AS DECIMAL) and consider a default rate when NULL.

stock_qty.description: Current stock quantity recorded in POS or inventory sync as text (e.g., '186.0').
stock_qty.analysis_use: Primary inventory metric used for alerts, reordering logic, stock valuation and reconciliation against sales. Must be cast to numeric for arithmetic and comparisons.
business_questions: Which SKUs have stock_qty below the reorder_point?
What is the total units in stock across the catalog?
stock_qty.nl2sql_hints: CAST/TRY_CAST to DECIMAL/INT for comparisons and sums. Watch for scaled values ('.0') and NULLs. Combine with uom to normalize units before summing across different measures.

reorder_point.description: Threshold quantity at which the product should be reordered (text numeric).
reorder_point.analysis_use: Compare against stock_qty to identify items needing reorder. Use for inventory health dashboards and generating purchase suggestions.
business_questions: Which SKUs are below their reorder_point and need reorder?
How many SKUs have a reorder_point greater than 50 units?
reorder_point.nl2sql_hints: Cast to numeric for comparisons. There may be default or missing values; handle NULLs defensively. Use simple WHERE CAST(stock_qty AS NUMERIC) <= CAST(reorder_point AS NUMERIC) for low-stock queries.

active_flag.description: Indicator if the product is active in POS catalog; stored as text '1.0' in this snapshot.
active_flag.analysis_use: Filter active vs inactive products for pricing and inventory operations. Because it's constant '1.0' here, it indicates all rows are active.
business_questions: How many active products are there in the POS catalog?
Show only active SKUs and their prices.
active_flag.nl2sql_hints: Normalize values (e.g., '1.0' -> '1' or boolean). Use equality checks like active_flag IN ('1', '1.0', 'true') or CAST logic. If creating boolean columns, map '1'/'0' to TRUE/FALSE.

updated_at.description: Timestamp string when the POS product record was last updated (e.g., '2025-11-05 11:45:22'). Stored as nvarchar.
updated_at.analysis_use: Temporal filtering and freshness checks, history tracking for change detection and incremental ingest. Useful for detecting stale products or recent price changes.
business_questions: Which SKUs were updated in the last 7 days?
What is the most recent update timestamp across all SKUs?
updated_at.nl2sql_hints: Convert to proper DATETIME/TIMESTAMP using TRY_CAST or PARSE (e.g., TRY_CAST(updated_at AS DATETIME)). Ensure consistent timezone handling. Use updated_at for incremental ingestion/WHERE updated_at > last_sync.

Unnamed: 12.description: Completely empty column from raw ingestion; no distinct values.
Unnamed: 12.analysis_use: None. Can be dropped during ETL. It does not contribute to analytics.
business_questions: Is there any data in this column?
Unnamed: 12.nl2sql_hints: Exclude from SELECT and ignore in joins. Remove in normalized schema to reduce noise.

Unnamed: 13.description: Completely empty column from raw ingestion; no distinct values.
Unnamed: 13.analysis_use: None. Candidate for removal during cleanup.
business_questions: Does this column contain any usable values?
Unnamed: 13.nl2sql_hints: Drop or ignore; do not use in queries or joins.

Unnamed: 14.description: Mostly-empty column containing only whitespace as top value; not meaningful.
Unnamed: 14.analysis_use: None practical. Possibly holds corrupted or placeholder values from source file.
business_questions: What values exist in this column and are they meaningful?
Should this column be removed during ETL?
Unnamed: 14.nl2sql_hints: Trim whitespace to reveal emptiness. If only blanks/NULLs, drop. If used, normalize values before use.

sku.description: Stock Keeping Unit identifier for the product. Stored as nvarchar and appears to be the product-level key in this extract.
sku.analysis_use: Used for grouping, filtering, ranking and joining to product master or sales/transaction tables to enrich with product metadata. Primary grouping dimension for all SKU-level aggregations.
business_questions: Which SKUs account for X% of total net sales?
Show the top N SKUs by net sales or gross sales.
sku.nl2sql_hints: Treat as a text dimension. Use exact matching or IN lists for filters. When joining, use canonical sku column from product master (watch for padding/case). Be careful about leading/trailing spaces; use RTRIM/LTRIM if needed. If queries expect SKU uniqueness, validate duplicates first before assuming PK behavior.

Gross Sales.description: Total gross sales amount for the SKU as captured in the raw extract. Currently stored as nvarchar with decimal-like strings.
Gross Sales.analysis_use: Primary measure for revenue analysis: sum, average, ranking, and contribution calculations. Often used as the denominator when computing discount% or tax% and for reconciliation against Net Sales.
business_questions: What is the total gross sales per SKU and across all SKUs?
Which SKUs have gross sales above a given threshold?
Gross Sales.nl2sql_hints: Must be cast to a numeric type for aggregations (e.g., TRY_CAST([Gross Sales] AS decimal(18,2)) or CAST(REPLACE([Gross Sales],',','') AS decimal(...)) if thousand separators expected). Column name has a space: use brackets/quoting or alias (e.g., [Gross Sales] AS gross_sales). Handle NULLs and malformed values with TRY_CAST/NULLIF/ISNUMERIC guards. For NL2SQL generation, prefer normalized column name gross_sales in output; include explicit casts in generated SQL.

Discount Amount.description: Total discount amount applied to the SKU. Stored as nvarchar, representing monetary values.
Discount Amount.analysis_use: Used to compute discount totals, average discount per SKU, discount as a percent of gross sales, and to analyze promotions/markdown impact. Useful in margin and profitability calculations.
business_questions: What is the total discount given per SKU and across SKUs?
Which SKUs have the highest discount as a percentage of gross sales?
Discount Amount.nl2sql_hints: Cast to numeric before aggregation (TRY_CAST or safe parsing). Be cautious of zero or NULL gross sales when computing discount percentages (guard divide-by-zero). Handle spaces in column name with brackets. Consider trimming and validating numeric text; for NL2SQL, generate expressions like COALESCE(TRY_CAST([Discount Amount] AS decimal(18,2)),0).

Tax Amount.description: Tax amount applied to the SKU (monetary). Stored as nvarchar in the raw extract.
Tax Amount.analysis_use: Used to calculate tax totals, tax share of gross sales, and to validate tax calculations. Can be aggregated to see tax liability by SKU when combined with jurisdictional mappings (if available elsewhere).
business_questions: How much tax was collected per SKU?
What percentage of gross sales is tax for each SKU?
Tax Amount.nl2sql_hints: Treat as numeric after casting (TRY_CAST/CAST). Use bracket quoting for column name. When generating NL2SQL, ensure safe numeric conversion and guard against nulls. If tax rate needs to be computed, use expression: tax_amount / NULLIF(gross_sales,0).

Net Sales.description: Net sales figure for the SKU after discounts and taxes as recorded in the extract. Stored as nvarchar with decimal-like format.
Net Sales.analysis_use: Key bottom-line measure: aggregate net sales by SKU, compute contribution to total net sales, and reconcile against Gross Sales minus Discount Amount plus Tax Amount. Used in performance ranking and revenue reporting.
business_questions: What are the top SKUs by net sales?
Does Net Sales equal Gross Sales - Discount Amount + Tax Amount for each SKU (reconciliation)?
Net Sales.nl2sql_hints: Cast to decimal for numeric operations. Use bracket quoting for the column name or alias in generated SQL. For reconciliation checks, produce queries like: CAST([Gross Sales] AS decimal(...)) - CAST([Discount Amount] AS decimal(...)) + CAST([Tax Amount] AS decimal(...)) AS computed_net and compare to CAST([Net Sales] AS decimal(...)). Use TRY_CAST to avoid query failures on malformed strings.

pos_return_id.description: Identifier for the POS return event / returned line. Appears unique per row in this snapshot (50 distinct values for 50 rows).
pos_return_id.analysis_use: Primary identifier for deduplication and linking return-level metadata. Useful for counting distinct returns, dedup checks, and auditing. Can be used to join to any external return ledger when available.
business_questions: How many distinct return records were processed during a period?
Are there duplicate pos_return_id values indicating ingestion or data quality issues?
pos_return_id.nl2sql_hints: Treat as string when matching; but can be used as a grouping key. Ensure exact-match comparisons (no implicit numeric casts). For performance, consider index on pos_return_id if used in lookups. Check for leading/trailing whitespace and consistent casing before equality joins.

pos_txn_id.description: Identifier for the originating POS transaction associated with the return (transaction-level key).
pos_txn_id.analysis_use: Join key to POS transaction/order tables to attribute returns to customers, stores, or sale metadata. Grouping by pos_txn_id can show multi-line returns per transaction. Filtering by transaction allows reconciling returns to sales.
business_questions: How many returns are associated with a single POS transaction (bulk returns)?
Which transactions generated the largest total refunds due to returns?
pos_txn_id.nl2sql_hints: Stored as nvarchar; when joining, match to the transaction table's equivalent string column (trim whitespace). Watch for partial matches; prefer equality on the full id. If queries filter by transaction patterns (e.g., 'POS100%'), use LIKE. Consider indexing on pos_txn_id for larger datasets.

return_ts.description: Timestamp when the return occurred (stored as nvarchar, values resemble 'YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss').
return_ts.analysis_use: Primary time dimension for event-time analyses: grouping by day/week/month, computing time-to-process, trend analysis. Must be parsed/cast to a datetime type for correct temporal functions and ordering.
business_questions: What is the daily count and refund total of returns?
Are there hourly patterns when returns spike during the day?
return_ts.nl2sql_hints: Cast/convert the string to TIMESTAMP or DATETIME in SQL (e.g., TRY_CAST/TRY_CONVERT) before grouping or filtering by date ranges. Validate format consistency; handle NULLs. Use parsed date for BETWEEN filters (avoid string comparisons which are error-prone).

sku.description: Stock Keeping Unit identifier of the returned item.
sku.analysis_use: Group and aggregate returns and refund amounts by SKU to find problem products. Join to product master tables to enrich with name, category, brand, and cost/price data.
business_questions: Which SKUs have the highest return rate or refund amount?
Are specific SKUs more likely to be returned for damage or wrong item?
sku.nl2sql_hints: Match to product tables using exact SKU string equality (trim/upper/lower as needed). Consider using SKU for GROUP BY and ORDER BY; for larger cardinalities, pre-aggregate. If SKU is stored in multiple tables with different formats, normalize casing and leading zeros before joins.

qty.description: Quantity of units returned for this SKU in the return event (stored as nvarchar; small integer-like values).
qty.analysis_use: Aggregate returned units (SUM) to compute total units returned by SKU, transaction, or period. Use in per-unit metrics (refund per unit). Must be cast to integer for arithmetic.
business_questions: How many units in total were returned for a given SKU or time window?
What is the average quantity per return event?
qty.nl2sql_hints: Convert to INTEGER/SMALLINT using safe casting functions (TRY_CAST/TRY_CONVERT) to avoid failures on bad data. When aggregating, coalesce NULLs to 0. Validate out-of-range values (negative or extremely large quantities).

reason.description: Categorical reason for the return (e.g., Wrong Item, Customer Return, Damaged).
reason.analysis_use: Group counts and refund totals by reason to identify top causes. Filter for specific reasons to analyze processing patterns (approval/restock rates by reason). Useful for root-cause and quality analytics.
business_questions: What are the top reasons for returns and their contribution to refund totals?
Which reasons require approval more often or lead to restocking?
reason.nl2sql_hints: Low-cardinality categorical field—use for GROUP BY. Normalize values (trim/case) to avoid duplicate categories. Consider creating a lookup table for standardized reasons if values vary over time.

refund_amount.description: Monetary amount refunded for this returned line (stored as nvarchar but decimal-like strings).
refund_amount.analysis_use: Aggregate SUM(refund_amount) for total refunds, compute averages, medians, and refund-per-unit metrics. Important for financial reconciliation and loss analysis. Must be parsed to numeric (DECIMAL) for correct summation and sorting.
business_questions: What is the total refund liability over a period, and which SKUs contribute most?
What is the average refund amount per return and per unit?
refund_amount.nl2sql_hints: Cast/convert to DECIMAL/NUMERIC (explicit precision/scale) using safe functions (TRY_CAST). Watch for string representations with extra precision (e.g., '129.90000000000001')—round as required for reporting. Use SUM, AVG on numeric cast values. Coalesce invalid/null to 0 if appropriate.

processed_by.description: Identifier for the POS user/processor who handled the return (e.g., PU1003).
processed_by.analysis_use: Attribute returns and refunds to staff for performance and anomaly detection. Group by processed_by to produce leaderboards, error rates, or training needs.
business_questions: Which POS users processed the most returns and the highest refund amounts?
Are there processors with unusually high damage/ wrong-item rates?
processed_by.nl2sql_hints: Use as a join key to pos_users or employee rosters (match on user code). Low cardinality—GROUP BY is efficient. Normalize strings before joins. Consider privacy/permission controls if exposing user-level performance.

approval_required_flag.description: Flag indicating whether the return required approval (stored as nvarchar '0'/'1').
approval_required_flag.analysis_use: Segment returns by approval requirement to measure workflow overhead, approval rates, and correlation with reasons or refund amounts. Use as filter or grouping dimension.
business_questions: What percentage of returns require approval and what is their total refund amount?
Do certain reasons or SKUs trigger approvals more frequently?
approval_required_flag.nl2sql_hints: Cast to BOOLEAN or SMALLINT for logical operations (e.g., approval_required_flag = '1' or after casting = 1). Normalize values (possible '0','1', 'Y','N') and handle NULLs. Use as WHERE or GROUP BY condition.

restock_flag.description: Flag indicating whether the returned item was restocked to inventory (stored as nvarchar '0'/'1').
restock_flag.analysis_use: Measure inventory impact of returns, restocking rates, and relationship with reason or damage. Combine with qty to compute net inventory changes.
business_questions: What share of returned items are restocked vs written off?
Which reasons (e.g., Damaged) have low restock rates and higher refund costs?
restock_flag.nl2sql_hints: Cast to BOOLEAN/SMALLINT for aggregation (SUM/restock counts). When computing net inventory change, multiply restock_flag cast by qty cast. Normalize flag values and treat NULLs explicitly.

created_at.description: Ingestion or record creation timestamp in the raw table (stored as nvarchar). May differ from return_ts which represents the event time.
created_at.analysis_use: Use for data freshness checks, late-arrival detection, and ingestion-time windowing. Useful in ETL pipelines to determine incremental loads and for auditing.
business_questions: When were return records ingested into the raw store and are there delays from the event time?
Are there batch arrival patterns in created_at that affect reporting latency?
created_at.nl2sql_hints: Cast to DATETIME/TIMESTAMP for comparisons and windowing. When computing event latency, subtract parsed return_ts from parsed created_at. Use TRY_CAST/TRY_CONVERT to avoid query failures on malformed strings.

date.description: Reporting date for the aggregated metrics. Stored as nvarchar(30) but values look like YYYY-MM-DD.
date.analysis_use: Primary time dimension for filtering and grouping (time series, period-to-date, monthly/yearly rollups). Should be cast to DATE for accurate date arithmetic and ordering.
business_questions: What were net sales by date for STORE_AHD_001 in January 2024?
Which dates had the top 10 highest gross_sales across all stores?
date.nl2sql_hints: Always CAST/CONVERT to DATE in generated SQL (e.g. TRY_CAST(date AS DATE) or TO_DATE). Handle invalid/malformed values and NULLs; use COALESCE or exclude rows where date is not a valid date. When filtering by date ranges prefer BETWEEN with casted dates for index/partition use.

store_id.description: Identifier for the store where the sales were recorded (3 distinct stores present).
store_id.analysis_use: Dimension for grouping, filtering, and joins to store metadata tables. Low cardinality makes it a good candidate for grouping and as a partition key in derived tables.
business_questions: Which store generated the highest total net_sales in Q4 2024?
What is the average total_qty_kg per day per store?
store_id.nl2sql_hints: Treat as a categorical string. When joining, prefer pos_stores.store_id; trim whitespace and ensure case-consistent comparison. Use exact equality filters (store_id = 'STORE_X') or IN lists for multi-store queries.

total_bills.description: Number of bills/transactions for the store on that date; currently stored as text.
total_bills.analysis_use: Aggregation (SUM, AVG) and rate calculations (average ticket = gross_sales/total_bills). CAST to INTEGER. Watch for non-numeric or empty strings.
business_questions: What is the average number of bills per day for each store?
Which date had the highest number of bills and how does that correlate with gross_sales?
total_bills.nl2sql_hints: Use TRY_CAST/SAFE_CAST to INT to avoid failures. Be prepared to handle zero or NULL when dividing (avoid division by zero). If total_bills has decimal strings, decide rounding policy (floor/round).

total_qty_kg.description: Total quantity sold in kilograms for the store on the date; stored as text and shows high cardinality.
total_qty_kg.analysis_use: Numeric metric for grouping and aggregation (SUM, AVG). Used to calculate sales per kg (gross_sales/total_qty_kg) and conversion to per-bill or per-customer metrics. CAST to DECIMAL/NUMERIC/DOUBLE.
business_questions: How many kilos were sold per day per store and what are the weekly trends?
Which store has higher sales per kg (revenue density)?
total_qty_kg.nl2sql_hints: CAST to DECIMAL or FLOAT using locale-insensitive parsing (replace commas if present). Filter out or NULL-out extreme outliers if they result from parsing errors. Use safe math guards for division (NULLIF(total_qty_kg,0)).

gross_sales.description: Gross sales amount before discounts/tax for the store and date; recorded as text with many near-unique values.
gross_sales.analysis_use: Primary financial metric for SUM, AVG, top-N, and trend analysis. Combine with discount/tax to reconcile to net_sales. CAST to DECIMAL and ensure consistent currency/scale.
business_questions: What are total gross_sales by store for the last 30 days?
How do gross_sales and discount_amount trend together during promotional periods?
gross_sales.nl2sql_hints: CAST to DECIMAL(18,2) or appropriate scale. Strip currency symbols and thousand separators. Use numeric casts in WHERE/HAVING clauses for performance. Consider indexing or clustering on (date, store_id) after casting for faster aggregations.

discount_amount.description: Total discount applied on that date for the store; stored as text.
discount_amount.analysis_use: Compute discount rate (discount_amount/gross_sales), trend discounts over time, and aggregate totals. CAST to DECIMAL and treat NULL/zero carefully.
business_questions: What is the average discount rate per store per month?
Which days had the largest absolute discount amounts and were they associated with promotions?
discount_amount.nl2sql_hints: CAST to DECIMAL. Be careful of negative values or formatting artifacts. When computing rates use NULLIF(gross_sales,0). Consider generating derived columns (discount_rate) in ETL for faster NL2SQL queries.

tax_amount.description: Total tax collected for the store on that date; stored as text.
tax_amount.analysis_use: Summation and average tax per bill, reconciling tax with gross/net sales, and tax reporting. CAST to DECIMAL.
business_questions: What are total tax collections by store for each month?
What is the average tax per bill for each store?
tax_amount.nl2sql_hints: CAST to DECIMAL. Validate expected tax ranges and ensure tax aligns with local tax rules. When producing NL2SQL responses, clarify whether tax is included in net_sales or separate.

net_sales.description: Net sales amount (post discounts and with tax treatment depending on source) for store and date; stored as text. Values show high cardinality and appear to be final revenue figures.
net_sales.analysis_use: Key KPI used for SUM, growth %, period-over-period comparisons, and ranking. CAST to DECIMAL and use for margin and contribution calculations when joined with cost data.
business_questions: What is the YoY or MoM growth in net_sales for each store?
Which days contributed to 80% of net_sales in a given month (Pareto)?
net_sales.nl2sql_hints: CAST to DECIMAL with correct precision. Clarify whether net_sales includes taxes; if net_sales calculation differs across systems, document transformation. Use safe division for growth rates with NULLIF(previous_value,0).

created_at.description: Ingestion or snapshot timestamp for the dataset; stored as nvarchar(48) and constant across rows (single distinct value).
created_at.analysis_use: Metadata: useful for data lineage, auditing, and knowing when the snapshot was taken. Not useful for time-series analysis of sales (use date instead).
business_questions: When was this POS daily snapshot ingested into the raw schema?
Has the ingestion time changed across successive extracts (useful for debugging late arrivals)?
created_at.nl2sql_hints: CAST to TIMESTAMP/DATETIME if needed. Because it's constant here, avoid using it in groupings. Use for data freshness checks (WHERE created_at > X) if multiple snapshots exist.

Unnamed: 9.description: Unused/empty artifact column from source ingestion — 100% NULL.
Unnamed: 9.analysis_use: None for analytics; drop from models and NL2SQL surface unless future source changes populate it.
business_questions: Is there any data in Unnamed: 9 to use for reporting? (No)
Can this column be safely dropped during ETL to reduce noise?
Unnamed: 9.nl2sql_hints: Exclude from SELECT lists in NL2SQL unless explicitly requested. Treat as nullable and typically drop in cleaned tables.

Unnamed: 10.description: Unused/empty artifact column from source ingestion — 100% NULL.
business_questions: Does Unnamed: 10 contain any useful metrics? (No)
Should this column be removed to simplify schemas?
Unnamed: 10.nl2sql_hints: Same as Unnamed: 9 — ignore/drop. If a user references it, explain that it contains no data.

Unnamed: 11.description: Sparse categorical artifact (99.8% NULL) with 4 small string values (e.g., 'da','gs','ns','ta') in a few rows — likely source flags or codes.
Unnamed: 11.analysis_use: Potentially useful if its meaning can be discovered (e.g., data source flag, status). Otherwise treat as optional categorical filter. Investigate provenance before use.
business_questions: What do the codes in Unnamed: 11 represent and which rows have them?
If these are status flags, how do flagged rows differ in net_sales or billing?
Unnamed: 11.nl2sql_hints: Treat as low-cardinality categorical; use WHERE Unnamed_11 IS NOT NULL to inspect. Do not assume it is a join key. Document in NL2SQL responses that this column is sparse and likely an artifact.

Unnamed: 12.description: Sparse numeric-like artifact (99.8% NULL) with 4 distinct large numeric values in a tiny number of rows — likely an aggregate or reference id from upstream.
Unnamed: 12.analysis_use: Investigate before use; could be a cumulative metric or identifier. Otherwise exclude from analytics to avoid skewing aggregates.
business_questions: What is the provenance of values in Unnamed: 12 and why are they present in only a few rows?
Are these values representing cumulative totals that should not be double-counted?
Unnamed: 12.nl2sql_hints: If used numerically, CAST to DECIMAL after validation. Prefer to drop or isolate these rows until meaning is confirmed. When writing NL2SQL, warn users that Unnamed:12 is sparse and may distort SUMs.

year.description: Calendar year for the aggregated row (stored as nvarchar). Currently all rows show '2024'.
year.analysis_use: Primary time filter for yearly selection and grouping. Often used with month to build a date key or period label for ordering and trend analysis.
business_questions: Filter the dataset to a specific year for year-over-year comparisons (when multiple years exist).
Limit KPI calculations to 2024 (or a selected year) for reporting and forecasting baselines.
year.nl2sql_hints: Treat as string in queries but cast to INT for numeric comparisons or ordering (CAST(year AS INT)). When constructing date keys, combine with month and cast/cast to date (e.g., make_date(year::int, month::int, 1) or CONCAT(year,'-',LPAD(month,2,'0'))). Beware of stray whitespace or non-numeric values in string storage.

month.description: Calendar month number for the aggregated row (stored as nvarchar). Values range 1-12.
month.analysis_use: Used for time-series grouping and ordering; combine with year to form a period. Useful for month-over-month growth, seasonality analysis, and monthly ranking.
business_questions: Show month-over-month net sales growth for a store.
Which months have the highest discount or tax proportions?
month.nl2sql_hints: Cast to INT for numeric ordering and range filters (CAST(month AS INT)). When concatenating into a date label, left-pad single-digit months to two digits (LPAD). Watch out for textual months — use TRIM() and CAST to ensure consistent behavior.

store_id.description: Identifier for the store where sales were recorded (nvarchar). Example values: STORE_AHD_01, STORE_SUR_02, STORE_VAD_03.
store_id.analysis_use: Grouping and filtering dimension for comparisons, joins to store master table for attributes (location, region), segmentation and per-store KPIs.
business_questions: Which store contributed the most to net sales in a given month?
Compare average ticket size or kg sold between two stores over the year.
store_id.nl2sql_hints: Use as join key to pos_stores or other sales tables. Ensure exact string matching (no implicit case changes) and handle NULLs. If matching to other tables where store ids have different casing/prefixes, normalize with UPPER()/TRIM().

total_bills.description: Total number of bills/transactions in the month for the store (stored as nvarchar but represents integer counts).
total_bills.analysis_use: Aggregate (SUM) and average (AVG per month/store) calculations; used to compute average ticket size (gross or net sales / total_bills) and transaction frequency.
business_questions: What is the average ticket value (net_sales / total_bills) per store per month?
How did transaction counts change month-over-month for each store?
total_bills.nl2sql_hints: Must be cast to INTEGER in SQL for numeric aggregations (CAST(TRIM(total_bills) AS INT) or TRY_CAST). Handle empty strings/NULLs with NULLIF or COALESCE. Avoid SUM on raw nvarchar to prevent implicit conversion errors.

total_qty_kg.description: Total quantity sold in kilograms for the month and store (stored as nvarchar representing decimal/float values).
total_qty_kg.analysis_use: SUM/AVG for volume-based KPIs, compute kg per bill, and correlate volume with revenue (price per kg). Useful for inventory planning and yield analysis.
business_questions: How many kg did each store sell in a given month?
What is the revenue per kg (net_sales / total_qty_kg) by store and month?
total_qty_kg.nl2sql_hints: Cast to DECIMAL or FLOAT before aggregation (CAST(REPLACE(TRIM(total_qty_kg), ',', '') AS DECIMAL(18,4))). Watch for locale formatting (commas) and trailing zeros. Use safe casting functions (TRY_CAST) if available to avoid query failures on malformed rows.

gross_sales.description: Gross sales amount for the month/store before discounts and taxes (stored as nvarchar decimal).
gross_sales.analysis_use: Primary revenue metric for top-line analysis, SUM/AVG for reporting, basis for discount and tax share computations and for calculating conversion metrics (gross per bill, gross per kg).
business_questions: What are monthly gross sales by store and how do they trend across the year?
Which store-month had the highest gross sales and what drove it (bills or quantity)?
gross_sales.nl2sql_hints: Must be cast to DECIMAL for SUM/AVG (CAST(gross_sales AS DECIMAL(18,2))). Beware of string formatting that prevents casting. When computing percentages (discount/gross), handle zero gross values to avoid divide-by-zero (CASE WHEN gross = 0 THEN NULL ELSE ...).

discount_amount.description: Total discounts applied for the month/store (stored as nvarchar decimal).
discount_amount.analysis_use: SUM/AVG to compute discount rates (discount/gross), trend discounting behavior, and measure promotion impact on revenue.
business_questions: What percentage of gross sales was given as discounts per store each month?
Did discount amounts increase during promotional months and what was the net sales impact?
discount_amount.nl2sql_hints: Cast to DECIMAL before aggregations. When computing proportions, use safe division with checks for zero gross_sales. If discount might be negative or formatted with parentheses, normalize strings (remove parentheses, replace commas) before casting.

tax_amount.description: Total tax collected for the month/store (stored as nvarchar decimal).
tax_amount.analysis_use: SUM/AVG for tax liability reporting, compute tax rate relative to gross or net sales, and reconciliation with finance records.
business_questions: How much tax did each store collect each month and what is the effective tax rate?
Are there months with anomalous tax amounts compared to gross sales?
tax_amount.nl2sql_hints: Cast to DECIMAL for numeric work. Be consistent whether tax is applied on gross or net in downstream calculations. Watch for localization/formatting issues (commas, negative signs).

net_sales.description: Net sales for the month/store after discounts and taxes (stored as nvarchar decimal).
net_sales.analysis_use: Primary bottom-line revenue metric for performance and KPI dashboards, SUM/AVG, margin calculations, month-over-month growth, and per-bill or per-kg metrics.
business_questions: What are monthly net sales per store and what is the month-over-month growth rate?
How does net sales per kg vary across stores and months?
net_sales.nl2sql_hints: Cast to DECIMAL for any aggregation or arithmetic. If net_sales should equal gross - discount +/− tax, consider validating by computing and flagging mismatches. Use NULL-safe arithmetic and formatting cleanup before CAST.

created_at.description: ETL or record creation timestamp stored as nvarchar. Appears constant in current snapshot (2025-12-02 10:42:54), likely the time the dataset was written.
created_at.analysis_use: Metadata for data lineage, freshness checks, and ETL monitoring rather than analytical grouping. Can be used to filter the latest load if there are multiple load timestamps in the future.
business_questions: When was this monthly rollup last updated (data freshness)?
Was there a reload or update at a specific created_at that could explain changes in reported numbers?
created_at.nl2sql_hints: Cast to DATETIME/TIMESTAMP for temporal comparisons (CAST(created_at AS DATETIME) or TRY_CAST). Because created_at is text and appears constant, don't rely on it for distinguishing monthly rows. If empty or malformed values exist, use TRY_CAST or ISDATE checks.

year.description: Reporting year for the aggregated metrics (stored as nvarchar). Represents the calendar year the metrics cover.
year.analysis_use: Used for filtering (WHERE year = '2024'), grouping (GROUP BY year), and joining to other year-level tables. Important for partitioning or selecting the timeframe of interest.
business_questions: Which year does a given row represent?
Filter metrics for a particular year (e.g., compare 2023 vs 2024) when multiple years are present.
year.nl2sql_hints: Treat as a year identifier string. Prefer casting to integer for numeric comparisons and sorting (CAST(year AS INT) or TRY_CAST). Watch for inconsistent formats (e.g., '2024', 'FY24'); normalize values before comparison. Use explicit equality instead of relying on implicit type conversions.

store_id.description: Identifier for the POS store (string). Likely corresponds to pos_stores.store_id or similar store dimension.
store_id.analysis_use: Primary grouping key for all store-level aggregates, filtering by store, joining to store metadata, and partitioning analyses across locations or regions.
business_questions: What are the yearly metrics for STORE_AHD_01?
Which stores contributed the top 3 net sales in the year?
store_id.nl2sql_hints: Use exact string equality for joins (e.g., ON pos_stores.store_id = pos_sales_yearly.store_id). Consider trimming/upper-casing if inconsistent formats exist. Note there is no surrogate key; if multiple tables use different store id formats, include normalization steps in queries.

total_bills.description: Total number of bills/transactions in the year for the store, stored as nvarchar but representing an integer count.
total_bills.analysis_use: Aggregate metric (SUM when analyzing multiple rows) or scalar for per-store analysis. Used in rate/average calculations (e.g., avg ticket = net_sales / total_bills). Useful for trend and throughput analyses.
business_questions: How many bills did each store process in 2024?
What is the average net sales per bill for each store?
total_bills.nl2sql_hints: CAST/TRY_CAST to INTEGER (or BIGINT) before arithmetic. Guard against NULLs or non-numeric strings using TRY_CAST or ISNUMERIC checks, and use COALESCE to provide defaults. Avoid string-based ordering when you need numeric order.

total_qty_kg.description: Total quantity sold in kilograms for the store in the year, stored as nvarchar representing a decimal value.
total_qty_kg.analysis_use: Used to measure volume sold, compute unit price equivalents (gross_sales / total_qty_kg), and compare physical throughput across stores. Can be summed across stores to get chain-wide volumes.
business_questions: Which store sold the largest quantity (kg) in 2024?
What is the average price per kg at each store (gross_sales / total_qty_kg)?
total_qty_kg.nl2sql_hints: CAST/TRY_CAST to DECIMAL/NUMERIC for accurate division and aggregation. Handle zero or NULL values to avoid division-by-zero errors; use NULLIF(total_qty_kg_cast,0) in denominators. Normalize decimal separators if data contains localized formats.

gross_sales.description: Gross sales amount (pre-discount / pre-tax) for the store in the year, stored as nvarchar representing a monetary decimal.
gross_sales.analysis_use: Primary revenue measure for comparisons, summation across stores, and percentage calculations (e.g., discount % = discount_amount / gross_sales). Used in ranking stores and computing revenue-based KPIs.
business_questions: What were the gross sales per store in 2024?
Which store had the highest gross to net delta (gross_sales - net_sales)?
gross_sales.nl2sql_hints: CAST/TRY_CAST to DECIMAL(18,2) or appropriate precision for monetary calculations. Use consistent currency/scale assumptions. When writing NL2SQL, prefer explicit numeric casts and aliasing (e.g., CAST(gross_sales AS DECIMAL(18,2)) AS gross_sales_dec). Avoid string comparisons or ordering on raw nvarchar.

discount_amount.description: Total discount amount applied for the store in the year, stored as nvarchar representing a monetary decimal.
discount_amount.analysis_use: Used to compute discount rates relative to gross_sales, evaluate promotional impact, and reconcile with accounting. Can be summed and compared across stores or time periods.
business_questions: How much discount did each store give in 2024?
What percentage of gross sales was given as discounts per store?
discount_amount.nl2sql_hints: CAST/TRY_CAST to DECIMAL and handle NULLs. Compute percentages safely using NULLIF(gross_sales_cast, 0) to avoid division by zero. Consider negative values or parentheses-formatted numbers in raw strings; normalize before casting.

tax_amount.description: Total tax amount collected for the store in the year, stored as nvarchar representing a decimal monetary value.
tax_amount.analysis_use: Used for tax reporting, computing net vs gross reconciliation, and assessing tax burden per store. Can be aggregated chain-wide or by region after joining store metadata.
business_questions: How much tax was collected by each store in 2024?
What is the effective tax rate (tax_amount / gross_sales) per store?
tax_amount.nl2sql_hints: CAST/TRY_CAST to DECIMAL, ensure consistent sign conventions. Use NULLIF when dividing by gross_sales. If tax is included vs excluded in gross_sales, document assumption; adjust calculations accordingly.

net_sales.description: Net sales amount (after discounts and taxes adjustments as defined by the business) for the store in the year, stored as nvarchar representing a monetary decimal.
net_sales.analysis_use: Primary KPI for revenue after adjustments; used for ranking, profit analysis (when joined with cost data), and computing average ticket/net per bill. Summable across stores to get total net revenue.
business_questions: What were the net sales per store in 2024?
What is the average net sales per bill at each store?
net_sales.nl2sql_hints: CAST/TRY_CAST to DECIMAL and ensure that net_sales = gross_sales +/- discount/tax as expected; validate with reconciliations (gross_sales - discount_amount + tax_amount or other business rule). Use explicit casting in NL2SQL expressions and avoid string math.

created_at.description: Timestamp when the row was created or last refreshed, stored as nvarchar in format 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS'.
created_at.analysis_use: Used for audit, determining freshness of the rollup, and filtering recent updates. Helpful in ETL troubleshooting and deciding which snapshot to use if multiple versions exist.
business_questions: When was this yearly rollup last generated?
Show rows created after a given timestamp for monitoring pipeline freshness.
created_at.nl2sql_hints: CAST/TRY_CAST to DATETIME/TIMESTAMP using the known format (e.g., TRY_CAST(created_at AS DATETIME)) before performing temporal comparisons. Beware of timezone assumptions. If created_at is always identical for all rows, use it as a snapshot identifier rather than a per-row temporal dimension.

shift_id.description: Unique identifier for the closed shift. Appears to be a per-row unique key (values like 'SHIFTCLS1000').
shift_id.analysis_use: Primary key-like identifier for selecting or grouping individual shift records; used for joins to other shift-level tables or logs.
business_questions: What are the details (start/end, totals) for a particular shift_id?
Are there duplicate shift_id values or multiple closure rows for the same shift?
shift_id.nl2sql_hints: Treat as a string key. Because distinct count == row count, consider using shift_id as PK. In NL2SQL map natural language 'shift' to shift_id when the user mentions a specific shift. Use equality on shift_id for direct lookups: WHERE shift_id = 'SHIFTCLS1001'.

terminal_id.description: Identifier of the POS terminal where the shift was closed (low cardinality: 4 terminals).
terminal_id.analysis_use: Common grouping/filtering dimension for terminal-level performance, joins to terminal metadata tables for store/location.
business_questions: Which terminal had the highest total sales over the last month?
How many discrepancies were recorded per terminal?
terminal_id.nl2sql_hints: Terminal values are strings (e.g., 'TERM-001-4'). Use equality or IN lists for filters. If user asks 'by terminal', generate GROUP BY terminal_id. Consider joining to pos_terminals on terminal_id to get names/locations; guard join keys as strings.

user_id.description: Identifier of the user/cashier who closed the shift (very low cardinality: 3 users).
user_id.analysis_use: Filter and group for per-user performance (sales, discrepancies). Useful as a dimension for accountability.
business_questions: Which cashier (user_id) had the highest sales per shift?
Which user had the most frequent cash discrepancies?
user_id.nl2sql_hints: User values are string codes (e.g., 'PU1003'). Map natural language references like 'cashier' to user_id. GROUP BY user_id for aggregations. Join to pos_users or user master table using user_id as string key.

shift_start_ts.description: Shift start timestamp stored as nvarchar in 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS' format (parseable ISO-like).
shift_start_ts.analysis_use: Filter by date/time ranges, extract date/hour for time-series grouping, compute shift duration with shift_end_ts after conversion to datetime.
business_questions: How many shifts started between 08:00 and 12:00 in the last week?
What is the average shift duration per terminal?
shift_start_ts.nl2sql_hints: Must be cast/converted to datetime in NL2SQL: e.g. TRY_CAST(shift_start_ts AS DATETIME) or CONVERT(DATETIME, shift_start_ts, 120). Use DATEPART/CAST to date for grouping (e.g., CAST(TRY_CONVERT(DATETIME, shift_start_ts) AS DATE)). When building SQL, guard with TRY/TRY_CAST or use ISDATE checks because column is nvarchar.

shift_end_ts.description: Shift end timestamp stored as nvarchar, same format as shift_start_ts.
shift_end_ts.analysis_use: Used with shift_start_ts to compute shift duration, end-of-shift reporting, and filtering by end time.
business_questions: Which shifts ended after midnight (overnight shifts)?
What is the average end time for shifts per user?
shift_end_ts.nl2sql_hints: Same guidance as shift_start_ts: convert to datetime before arithmetic. Example: DATEDIFF(MINUTE, TRY_CAST(shift_start_ts AS DATETIME), TRY_CAST(shift_end_ts AS DATETIME)) as shift_minutes. Use TRY_CAST/TRY_CONVERT to avoid runtime errors on malformed strings.

total_sales.description: Total sales amount for the shift stored as nvarchar with decimal values.
total_sales.analysis_use: Aggregate (SUM, AVG), filter (>=, <=), compute ratios (refunds/total_sales) and compare across dimensions.
business_questions: What is total sales per day / per terminal / per user?
What is the average total_sales for shifts with discrepancies?
total_sales.nl2sql_hints: Always convert to numeric: CAST(total_sales AS DECIMAL(18,2)) or TRY_CAST(total_sales AS DECIMAL(18,2)). Use COALESCE/ISNULL to handle nulls. When building NL2SQL, map language 'sales amount' to this column and prefer numeric comparisons after casting.

cash_collected.description: Amount of cash collected during the shift (nvarchar storing decimal).
cash_collected.analysis_use: Used to compute cash share of sales, reconcile expected_cash, and detect shortages/overages.
business_questions: What percent of sales was collected in cash vs card per shift?
Which shifts show large differences between expected_cash and cash_collected?
cash_collected.nl2sql_hints: Convert to numeric before arithmetic. For cash reconciliation consider using expected_cash - counted_cash to compute discrepancy; cash_collected may differ from expected_cash if refunds/voids occurred. Use coercion functions and handle nulls safely.

card_collected.description: Amount collected via card payments during the shift (nvarchar decimal).
card_collected.analysis_use: Used with cash_collected to compute payment mix, aggregate card volume by user/terminal/time.
business_questions: How much card revenue was processed per terminal today?
What is the trend of card_collected over the last month?
card_collected.nl2sql_hints: Cast to DECIMAL for sums/averages. When calculating 'card share' use CAST(card_collected AS DECIMAL) / NULLIF(CAST(total_sales AS DECIMAL),0). Watch out for rounding and use appropriate precision (DECIMAL(18,2) or DECIMAL(18,4) depending on needs).

refunds_amt.description: Total refunds amount during the shift (nvarchar decimal).
refunds_amt.analysis_use: Measure refunds impact, compute refunds as percent of sales, identify high-refund shifts for fraud or quality issues.
business_questions: Which shifts had refunds exceeding 5% of total_sales?
Which cashier processed the most refunds in value?
refunds_amt.nl2sql_hints: Cast to numeric for ratio and threshold checks. If refunds can be negative or positive depending on sign conventions, confirm sign semantics before computing net sales. Use COALESCE(TRY_CAST(refunds_amt AS DECIMAL(18,2)),0).

expected_cash.description: System-calculated expected cash on hand at shift close (nvarchar decimal).
expected_cash.analysis_use: Primary value for cash reconciliation; compare expected_cash to counted_cash to find discrepancies.
business_questions: Which shifts show expected_cash differing from counted_cash and by how much?
What is the distribution of expected_cash per terminal?
expected_cash.nl2sql_hints: Cast to numeric for comparisons. For discrepancy compute: CAST(counted_cash AS DECIMAL) - CAST(expected_cash AS DECIMAL). Use NULLIF when dividing and TRY_CAST or TRY_CONVERT for safe parsing.

counted_cash.description: Actual cash counted in the drawer at shift closure (nvarchar decimal).
counted_cash.analysis_use: Used together with expected_cash to compute over/short amounts; used in audit and variance reporting.
business_questions: What is the average over/short amount per cashier?
How often are counted_cash amounts greater than expected_cash?
counted_cash.nl2sql_hints: Cast to DECIMAL for arithmetic. When user asks for 'discrepancy' compute COUNT/CATEGORY of (counted_cash - expected_cash) buckets. Use ABS for absolute discrepancy. Use TRY_CAST and handle NULLs.

discrepancies_note.description: Free-text note about discrepancies (nvarchar 16). Currently 100% NULL in sample. Intended to capture short explanation for a variance.
discrepancies_note.analysis_use: When populated, used as contextual metadata for rows with discrepancies; useful for qualitative analysis or rule-based categorizations (e.g., 'till short', 'customer refund').
business_questions: Which discrepancy reasons occur most often and what is their average discrepancy amount?
Show shifts where a discrepancy note is provided and list the note with the over/short amount.
discrepancies_note.nl2sql_hints: Because this is short free text and often null, include IS NOT NULL checks when user requests textual notes. For NL2SQL prefer LIKE or equality when matching specific reasons (e.g., WHERE discrepancies_note LIKE '%short%'). Consider trimming and normalizing text before using as filter.

store_id.description: Unique identifier for a POS store (string, e.g., 'STORE_AHD_001').
store_id.analysis_use: Primary reference/join key to map transactions, inventories and terminals to a store; grouping and counting stores; filtering to a subset of stores.
business_questions: Which transactions belong to store STORE_AHD_001 over the last month?
List all active store_ids and their last_online timestamps.
store_id.nl2sql_hints: Although not declared as a PK, treat store_id as the canonical join key to POS tables. Match exact string values (use equality and IN lists). Watch for whitespace/case differences; consider trimming and normalizing. If performance is required create an index or declare PRIMARY KEY in DDL. Typical NL2SQL patterns: WHERE store_id = 'STORE_X' or WHERE store_id IN ('STORE_A','STORE_B').

city.description: City where the store is located (string, e.g., 'Ahmedabad').
city.analysis_use: Use for grouping, aggregation and filtering by geography (city-level metrics). Useful for geospatial rollups and city-based dashboards.
business_questions: How many stores are in each city?
Show stores in Ahmedabad and their last_online times.
city.nl2sql_hints: Treat as categorical text. Use equality or GROUP BY city. Consider normalizing city strings for consistent matching. For NL2SQL, common clauses: GROUP BY city ORDER BY count DESC; WHERE city = 'Ahmedabad'.

region.description: Administrative region or area for the store (string). In this dataset values mirror city names but can represent broader regions.
region.analysis_use: Rollups and filtering at region level (aggregation, comparing region performance), dimension for regional hierarchies when joined with other data.
business_questions: How many stores are active per region?
Which regions have stores that have not been online in the last 30 days?
region.nl2sql_hints: Use as a GROUP BY / filter dimension. Verify whether region duplicates city; if region values are broader categories you may need mapping. Use WHERE region = 'RegionName' or GROUP BY region. Normalize empty/null region values (IS NULL) in NL2SQL queries.

first_active_date.description: Timestamp when the store was first activated (stored as nvarchar in format 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS').
first_active_date.analysis_use: Used for lifecycle analyses (store age), cohorts by activation period, filtering stores activated before/after a date, and computing tenure by casting to datetime and subtracting from current date.
business_questions: Which stores were activated in Q3 2025?
What is the average store age (in days) since first_active_date?
first_active_date.nl2sql_hints: Column is nvarchar — always CAST/CONVERT to datetime in WHERE, ORDER BY or DATEDIFF operations (e.g., CAST(first_active_date AS datetime) or TRY_CAST depending on DB). Handle NULLs and invalid strings. NL2SQL should generate explicit casts for date comparisons and use format-aware parsing; e.g., WHERE CAST(first_active_date AS datetime) >= '2025-09-01'. If underlying DB supports it, recommend migrating to native datetime type for efficient filtering and indexing.

last_online.description: Last time the store reported online/heartbeat (stored as nvarchar in format 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS').
last_online.analysis_use: Recency and uptime analysis: filtering stores by last seen, computing time since last online, detecting inactive stores, and ordering stores by most/least recently online. Useful as a health metric when joined to operational alerts.
business_questions: Which stores have not been online in the last 7 days?
List stores ordered by last_online descending to show most recently active stores.
last_online.nl2sql_hints: Also stored as text — CAST/CONVERT to datetime for accurate comparisons and range filters. Handle NULL values (e.g., WHERE last_online IS NULL). For recency calculations use DATEDIFF or equivalent on CAST(last_online AS datetime). NL2SQL templates should include defensive casts (TRY_CAST) if source strings can be malformed and ensure time zone considerations if applicable.

sync_id.description: Unique identifier assigned to each sync event (e.g., 'SYNC1000'). Appears to be an event id but not enforced as a primary key in DDL.
sync_id.analysis_use: Used for deduplication, identifying specific sync attempts, joins to any event-level logs, and ordering events. Good for selecting a single sync event or filtering by id range/pattern.
business_questions: Show details for sync_id = 'SYNC1003'.
Was sync_id X retried more than N times?
sync_id.nl2sql_hints: Treat as a string key. If uniqueness is required, check duplicates before using as PK. Use WHERE sync_id = '...'. For pattern matching use LIKE 'SYNC%' or LEFT(sync_id,4) = 'SYNC'. Avoid casting unless upstream changes make it numeric.

terminal_id.description: Identifier of the POS terminal performing the sync (e.g., 'TERM-001-2'). Low cardinality in sample.
terminal_id.analysis_use: Primary dimension for grouping and filtering. Use for per-terminal aggregates (success rate, avg records, last seen). Useful in WHERE or GROUP BY clauses and for joins to pos_terminals to fetch metadata.
business_questions: Which terminal has the most failed syncs in the last 7 days?
What is the average records_sent per sync for terminal TERM-001-2?
terminal_id.nl2sql_hints: Use exact equality joins/filters (terminal_id = 'TERM-001-2'). Because cardinality is low, GROUP BY terminal_id will return small result sets. Consider indexing terminal_id in the DB or joining to pos_terminals on terminal_id. Beware of trailing/leading spaces — trim values in predicates (LTRIM/RTRIM) if data quality issues exist.

sync_ts.description: Timestamp when the sync attempt occurred, stored as nvarchar in 'YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss' format in sample values.
sync_ts.analysis_use: Primary time dimension for trend analysis and windowing. Used to filter by time ranges, compute durations between attempts, bucket by day/hour, and order events. Must be cast to a datetime type for accurate temporal queries.
business_questions: How many syncs occurred each day for the last month?
What times of day do most failed syncs occur?
sync_ts.nl2sql_hints: Cast to datetime using TRY_CONVERT/TRY_CAST or CONVERT with style 120 (SQL Server): TRY_CONVERT(datetime2, sync_ts, 120). Filter using casted value, e.g., WHERE TRY_CONVERT(date, sync_ts) = '2025-11-22'. For performance, consider adding a persisted computed datetime column and index it. Handle invalid/malformed strings with TRY_* functions to avoid errors.

sync_status.description: Result of the sync attempt: e.g., 'SUCCESS' or 'FAILED'. Low distinct values (2).
sync_status.analysis_use: Used for calculating success/failure rates, filtering error cases, and for conditional aggregations (COUNT CASE WHEN sync_status='FAILED'). Good dimension for dashboards and KPIs.
business_questions: What percentage of syncs are successful vs failed over the last 30 days?
List all failed syncs and their associated error messages.
sync_status.nl2sql_hints: Treat as categorical string. For reliable NL2SQL parsing map synonyms ('success','ok','failed','error') to canonical terms. Use WHERE sync_status = 'FAILED' or CASE expressions. Consider normalizing values and adding a CHECK constraint or an enumerated type in the DB to avoid typos.

records_sent.description: Count of records sent from the terminal to central system during this sync, stored as nvarchar (sample values look numeric).
records_sent.analysis_use: Aggregate (SUM/AVG), min/max for throughput analysis, compare to records_received to detect discrepancies. Requires casting to integer for numeric aggregation.
business_questions: What is the total number of records sent by all terminals today?
Which sync had the highest records_sent in the last week?
records_sent.nl2sql_hints: Use TRY_CAST(records_sent AS int) or CAST after validating; handle NULLs with COALESCE/ISNULL. Use numeric casting in SELECT and GROUP BY only when necessary (group by the original string is possible but not recommended). Consider converting column to INT in the schema for better NL2SQL performance and to avoid runtime casts.

records_received.description: Count of records received by the terminal (or central system?) during the sync; stored as nvarchar but contains numeric values in sample.
records_received.analysis_use: Used similarly to records_sent to validate round-trips, detect loss/duplication (records_sent - records_received), and compute transfer success ratios. Requires casting to numeric type for arithmetic.
business_questions: Are there systematic differences between records_sent and records_received by terminal?
Identify syncs where records_received < records_sent.
records_received.nl2sql_hints: Cast to integer with TRY_CAST(records_received AS int) in queries. For difference calculations use TRY_CAST(records_sent AS int) - TRY_CAST(records_received AS int). Consider materializing a numeric column to avoid repeated casting.

error_message.description: Short text describing error when a sync fails. In sample most rows are NULL and all non-nulls are 'Network error'.
error_message.analysis_use: Group by error_message to identify common failure reasons, filter failed syncs by root cause, and drive alerting/triage. Many NULLs mean use COALESCE/IS NOT NULL filters.
business_questions: What are the top error messages causing failed syncs?
How many failed syncs with 'Network error' occurred in the last 24 hours?
error_message.nl2sql_hints: Treat as nullable string. Use WHERE error_message IS NOT NULL and GROUP BY error_message. Normalize similar messages (uppercase/lowercase, trimmed). Beware long text — use LEFT(error_message, N) in result sets if UI space is limited.

retry_count.description: Number of retry attempts for this sync recorded as nvarchar with small distinct set (0-3).
retry_count.analysis_use: Analyze retry patterns (distribution), correlate retry_count with success/failure, and compute average retries per terminal. Cast to integer for numeric aggregates.
business_questions: What proportion of failed syncs required >2 retries?
Which terminals have high average retry counts?
retry_count.nl2sql_hints: Use TRY_CAST(retry_count AS int) for numeric operations. Consider creating a smallint/integer column or a derived column for faster queries. Use filters like WHERE TRY_CAST(retry_count AS int) > 2. Consider treating retry_count NULL as 0 if business logic requires.

last_success_ts.description: Timestamp of the most recent successful sync for the terminal (string in sample). Useful to determine last-known good state per terminal.
last_success_ts.analysis_use: Compute time-since-last-success per terminal (NOW - last_success_ts), identify stale/offline terminals, and rank terminals by recency. Requires casting to datetime.
business_questions: When was the last successful sync for each terminal?
Which terminals have not had a successful sync in the last 24 hours?
last_success_ts.nl2sql_hints: Cast string to datetime: TRY_CONVERT(datetime2, last_success_ts, 120). For per-terminal latest use window functions or GROUP BY terminal_id with MAX(TRY_CONVERT(datetime2, last_success_ts, 120)). Consider populating/maintaining a separate per-terminal 'last_seen' table or indexed computed column for performant queries.

created_at.description: Timestamp when the log row was created in the system (string format in sample). Can be used as ingestion/time of record arrival.
created_at.analysis_use: Use for ingestion-time filtering, ordering, and for comparing to sync_ts to detect ingestion delays. Useful for incremental extraction and for time-bounded NL2SQL queries.
business_questions: How long after sync_ts are sync logs created on average?
Show sync logs created in the last hour.
created_at.nl2sql_hints: Convert to datetime with TRY_CONVERT/TRY_CAST for filtering and date arithmetic. For incremental ETL use WHERE created_at > @last_run_casted. Consider adding a proper datetime column or an index on a persisted computed datetime for performance and predictable NL2SQL translation.

terminal_id.description: Logical identifier for the POS terminal (tenant-specific id like TERM-001-2).
terminal_id.analysis_use: Primary identifier for grouping, deduplication and linking to terminal-level activity. Useful for counting unique terminals, latest-last_online per terminal, time-series of terminal events when joined to event/transaction tables.
business_questions: How many unique terminals are active in the fleet?
What is the last online timestamp per terminal (identify stale terminals)?
Which terminal_ids correspond to terminals generating the most transactions? (when joined)
terminal_id.nl2sql_hints: Treat as string key. When joining, prefer matching on terminal_id if other tables store it; otherwise use terminal_code or device_serial. Use GROUP BY terminal_id for per-terminal aggregates. Beware of inconsistent casing/whitespace; use TRIM()/LOWER() if necessary.

store_id.description: Identifier of the store where the terminal is located (e.g., STORE_AHD_001).
store_id.analysis_use: Common filter and GROUP BY key for per-store summaries (terminal counts, software coverage, last-online health). Primary join key to pos_stores to enrich with store name, region, timezone, etc.
business_questions: How many terminals are installed in each store?
Which stores have terminals that haven't checked in recently?
What stores still run older software versions?
store_id.nl2sql_hints: Stored as nvarchar—use exact string equality for joins. Join to zuna_seed_raw.pos_stores or pos_stores in schema: ON pos_terminals.store_id = pos_stores.store_id. Consider normalizing whitespace and case for robust joins.

terminal_code.description: Human-friendly code for the terminal within a store (e.g., STORE_AH-T1).
terminal_code.analysis_use: Useful for UI-level reporting, mapping terminals to cashier-facing identifiers, and linking to transaction-level tables if they use code. Can be grouped for intra-store terminal comparisons.
business_questions: Which terminal codes at STORE_AHD_001 are producing the most transactions?
Which terminal_code is assigned to which user currently?
terminal_code.nl2sql_hints: May duplicate terminal_id; check which key other tables reference (terminal_code vs terminal_id). Treat as string; use it in filters for user-facing queries. Consider TRIM() if codes have trailing spaces.

device_serial.description: Hardware serial number of terminal device (vendor-provided SN).
device_serial.analysis_use: Useful to identify physical units across repairs, replacements, and vendor warranty lookups. Join key to hardware inventory or vendor partner tables if available.
business_questions: Which device serials have required maintenance or replacements?
Are there multiple terminal records mapped to the same device_serial (device reassignments)?
device_serial.nl2sql_hints: Treat as string; may be unique. Use for joins to any hardware inventory or vendor tables. Watch for different serial formatting; canonicalize before joins if necessary.

pos_software_version.description: POS application version installed on the terminal (text).
pos_software_version.analysis_use: Used for rollout tracking, compliance checks, and filtering devices needing upgrade. Low cardinality typically; used in GROUP BY to compute coverage percentages.
business_questions: What proportion of terminals run version v3.2.1?
Which stores have terminals not yet upgraded to the target version?
pos_software_version.nl2sql_hints: String column; compare literal versions. Because sample shows single value, watch for sparse variation. For semantic version comparisons, parse version string into components (major/minor/patch) for numeric comparisons.

assigned_user_id.description: Identifier of the user/operator assigned to the terminal (e.g., PU1001).
assigned_user_id.analysis_use: Join key to pos_users for operator details, used for accountability reports and to find terminals per operator. Filter/group to see user assignments or reassignments over time when combined with history.
business_questions: Which terminals is PU1001 assigned to?
How many terminals are currently unassigned vs assigned per user?
assigned_user_id.nl2sql_hints: String ID used to join to pos_users: ON pos_terminals.assigned_user_id = pos_users.user_id (or equivalent). Expect NULLs for unassigned; include IS NULL handling. Use exact equality for filters and consider normalizing casing.

last_online_ts.description: Timestamp (as string) when the terminal last checked in or was seen online (format: 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MI:SS').
last_online_ts.analysis_use: Primary signal for availability/health — used for recency filters, SLA/uptime calculations, identifying offline devices, and computing time-since-last-contact aggregations.
business_questions: Which terminals have not been online in the last 24/48/72 hours?
What is the distribution of time-since-last-online across terminals?
last_online_ts.nl2sql_hints: Stored as nvarchar—convert to datetime for comparisons. In SQL Server use TRY_CONVERT(datetime, last_online_ts, 120) or CONVERT(datetime, last_online_ts, 120); in other DBs use CAST/TO_TIMESTAMP with correct format. Handle invalid/NULL values with TRY_/NULLIF patterns. When writing NL2SQL, prefer explicit casting and ISO format to avoid locale issues.

status.description: Operational status of the terminal (e.g., ACTIVE, INACTIVE).
status.analysis_use: Filter and segment devices by operational state for summaries and alerts. Used to exclude decommissioned devices from active counts.
business_questions: How many terminals are ACTIVE vs INACTIVE?
Which ACTIVE terminals have not reported in the last 24 hours?
status.nl2sql_hints: String values (case-sensitive depending on DB). In this sample it's constant 'ACTIVE' — expect more states in production. Use WHERE status = 'ACTIVE' or IN (...) for multi-state filters. Normalize case if source is inconsistent.

location_lat.description: Latitude of terminal location stored as text (decimal string).
location_lat.analysis_use: Map terminals spatially, cluster by geography, compute distances between terminals and stores, and create heatmaps. Cast to numeric for calculations and geospatial indexing if supported.
business_questions: What are the geographic coordinates of terminals in STORE_SUR_001?
Which terminals are within X km of a reference point (e.g., distribution center)?
location_lat.nl2sql_hints: Convert to numeric: TRY_CAST(location_lat AS decimal(10,7)) or similar. Handle trailing/extra precision (examples show long decimal strings). Use both location_lat and location_long together for geospatial queries; NULLs will break distance calculations so filter them out.

location_long.description: Longitude of terminal location stored as text (decimal string).
location_long.analysis_use: Paired with location_lat for mapping and spatial calculations; useful for region-based filtering and visualizations.
business_questions: Show terminal locations on a map for a selected city/region.
Which terminals are located within a delivery area polygon?
location_long.nl2sql_hints: Same as location_lat—cast to decimal/float before numeric operations: TRY_CAST(location_long AS decimal(11,7)). Combine with location_lat for geospatial calculations. Consider normalizing extreme precision (rounding) for grouping.

created_at.description: Creation/provision timestamp of the terminal record (as string).
created_at.analysis_use: Onboarding and lifecycle analysis: compute how long a terminal has been in service, cohort analysis by provisioning date, filter terminals added during a rollout period.
business_questions: How many terminals were provisioned in the last quarter?
What is the average age (in days) of terminals in the fleet?
created_at.nl2sql_hints: Stored as nvarchar—convert to datetime (TRY_CONVERT/CONVERT or CAST/TO_TIMESTAMP depending on engine). Use date functions to compute age or cohorts. Watch for NULLs and invalid strings; use TRY_/safe parsing to avoid query failures.

pos_line_id.description: Identifier for the POS transaction line; appears unique per row and represents the line-level id on a receipt.
pos_line_id.analysis_use: Use as a row-level unique identifier (de-duplication). Useful for counting distinct lines, tracing specific line items, and joining if other tables reference this exact id.
business_questions: How many line items were recorded in total?
Can we identify or de-duplicate duplicate line records by pos_line_id?
pos_line_id.nl2sql_hints: Treat as string/varchar. Because there is no declared primary key, do not assume referential integrity. Use pos_line_id for selection or exact-match filters. If performing joins, confirm target table uses the same string encoding/trim and consistent casing.

pos_txn_id.description: Identifier of the parent POS transaction/receipt containing this line. Multiple lines map to the same transaction.
pos_txn_id.analysis_use: Group by pos_txn_id to compute lines per transaction, transaction-level totals (SUM(line_total)), and join to pos_transactions to get transaction metadata (store, terminal, cashier, timestamp).
business_questions: How many lines does each POS transaction contain?
What is the total value per transaction (sum of line_total) and how does it compare with transaction-level records?
pos_txn_id.nl2sql_hints: Stored as string. When joining to pos_transactions, ensure matching data types and trimming. Aggregations across transactions should use GROUP BY pos_txn_id. Be careful: POS transaction id cardinality is low vs lines (multiple lines per txn).

sku.description: Stock keeping unit code for the product sold on this line. Low cardinality with a few SKUs dominating.
sku.analysis_use: Primary grouping dimension for sales analytics: units sold (SUM(qty)), revenue (SUM(line_total)), average price. Use to join with product master tables for product name/category/brand.
business_questions: Which SKUs are top-sellers by units and revenue?
What is the price and revenue distribution per SKU?
sku.nl2sql_hints: Stored as string and can be joined to products or pos_products. Normalize case and whitespace before joining. Expect low cardinality—use exact equality or IN lists for filters. If matching to product_variants, prefer left join in NL2SQL when product master may lack rows.

qty.description: Quantity sold on the line. Stored as nvarchar but appears integer-like (small ints).
qty.analysis_use: Numeric aggregation: SUM(qty) for units sold, AVG(qty) for typical line quantity. Use in revenue per unit computations (line_total / qty) after casting to numeric.
business_questions: How many total units of a SKU were sold (sum of qty)?
What is the typical quantity per line (average qty) and distribution (counts by qty)?
qty.nl2sql_hints: Must cast to integer (e.g., CAST(qty AS INT)) or numeric; guard for null/empty strings. Use TRY_CAST/SAFE_CAST to avoid errors if input is malformed. When used in WHERE clauses, compare after casting or ensure numeric literals are compared as strings with caution.

unit_price.description: Unit price applied for the line. Stored as string but numeric-like (several repeated values).
unit_price.analysis_use: Compute revenue consistency checks, detect price variations, average unit price per SKU, and compare with product price lists (joins). Use in derived calculations: expected_line_total = qty * unit_price.
business_questions: What is the average unit price for a SKU over the dataset?
Are there price discrepancies between unit_price * qty and recorded line_total?
unit_price.nl2sql_hints: Cast to numeric/decimal before arithmetic (e.g., CAST(unit_price AS DECIMAL(18,2))). Be mindful of currency and scaling. Use numeric casts in WHERE and GROUP BY for accurate grouping by price bands.

line_total.description: Total amount for the line (qty * unit_price minus discounts plus taxes if included). Stored as a string; many distinct values.
line_total.analysis_use: Primary revenue measure: SUM(CAST(line_total AS DECIMAL)) for total revenue, per-SKU revenue, per-transaction totals, and reconciliation with transaction totals. Use to compute per-unit realized price (line_total/qty).
business_questions: What is the total revenue generated by these POS lines?
Which lines or SKUs contribute most to revenue and how does line_total compare to expected amounts?
line_total.nl2sql_hints: Cast to decimal/float for accurate sums. Watch out for floating-point textual artifacts in tax_amt and line_total; prefer DECIMAL for financial sums. Use ROUND when presenting results. When reconciling, allow small tolerances (e.g., absolute difference) due to rounding.

discount_amt.description: Discount amount applied on the line. In this dataset it is consistently '0' for all rows.
discount_amt.analysis_use: Normally used to compute net revenue after discounts (line_total - discount_amt). Here it provides no variation; include but expect zero effect. Useful to flag if discounts appear in the future.
business_questions: How much discount was given per line and in aggregate?
Which SKUs or transactions received discounts?
discount_amt.nl2sql_hints: Stored as string; cast to numeric if used. Since values are all '0', queries filtering for discount_amt != 0 will return empty in current data — handle as potential historical/streaming field. Use COALESCE/NULLIF to treat empty strings as zero.

tax_amt.description: Tax amount applied to the line. Stored as string with decimal values and occasional floating-string artifacts (e.g., '4.7999999999999998').
